headline: Google DeepMind and Boston Dynamics advance embodied AI reasoning capabilities
body: Boston Dynamics and Google DeepMind have demonstrated new reasoning capabilities in Spot, the mobile robot, marking progress in embodied AI systems. This development arrives alongside Google's broader hardware push, including new chip designs to compete with Nvidia and potential partnerships with companies like Marvell for AI inference. For healthcare settings, embodied AI that can reason about physical environments has direct implications for autonomous systems in surgical suites, hospital logistics, and patient monitoring. As Google expands its AI inference infrastructure and chip capabilities, healthcare organizations should monitor how these investments shape the cost and deployment timeline for AI-powered robotic systems in clinical workflows. Vendors building healthcare-specific AI solutions will need to track whether Google's hardware plays become cost-competitive alternatives to current options.
